delete_confirm_message: This action cannot be undone. Are you sure you want to delete?
settings_description: Manage application appearance and data storage information.
choose_language: Choose the display language for the application.
storage_description: Currently in local mode. All API Keys, Skills, and MCP configurations are saved locally. Configure encrypted sync to remote object storage below.
sync_description: Encrypt local data and sync it to S3 (or compatible) object storage, then restore it on a new device. Desktop app only.
sync_desktop_only: Data sync is only available in the desktop app.
sync_passphrase_ph: Derives the AES-256 key; never uploaded
sync_passphrase_hint: The passphrase powers end-to-end encryption. Back it up safely — if lost, remote data cannot be decrypted.
sync_not_configured: Fill in bucket, credentials and a passphrase first.
sync_restore_confirm_desc: This overwrites the matching local resources with the remote backup and cannot be undone. Consider running a sync first.
sync_history_desc: Every backup keeps a timestamped snapshot. Restore from an older version if a backup overwrote configuration you need.
sync_history_empty: No history snapshots yet. Run a sync to create one.
sync_history_restore_confirm_desc: This overwrites the matching local resources with the selected snapshot and cannot be undone.
feature_config_description: Edit the local feature JSON. Production builds restart after saving.
ssh_page_desc: Manage SSH hosts with accounts, passwords and private keys in one place. Import ~/.ssh/config in one click; credentials are encrypted via the OS keychain and you can open a remote terminal instantly.
ssh_empty_desc: Add a host, or import from this machine's ~/.ssh/config. Importing reads and encrypts the referenced private key files so they are fully managed.
ssh_dialog_desc: Password, private key and passphrase are encrypted via the OS keychain and never written in plaintext.
ssh_key_required: Paste or read a private key
ssh_agent_hint: Authenticates using identities loaded in your local ssh-agent.
ssh_import_desc: Parse hosts from ~/.ssh/config; referenced private key files are read and encrypted into the managed vault.
ssh_vault_export_desc: Encrypt all SSH hosts (including decrypted credentials) with a passphrase into a portable .ltfvault file.
ssh_vault_import_desc: Pick a .ltfvault file and enter the passphrase used at export time to restore hosts.
ssh_vault_passphrase_hint: The passphrase is never stored. Keep it safe — losing it makes the file undecryptable.
ssh_desktop_only: SSH features are only available in the desktop app.
update_available_desc: Version {{version}} is available (you have {{current}}). Update now?
tools_description: Common encoding/decoding and text processing tools. All calculations are done locally.
browser_description: Built-in browser for previewing generated HTML artifacts or a local dev server.
browser_address_placeholder: Enter a URL or local address, e.g. localhost:5173
browser_empty_hint: Type a URL in the address bar above, or pick a common local address to start previewing.
browser_desktop_only_desc: The built-in browser uses a native webview and is only available in the desktop app (Tauri). Please open it there.
agent_resize_preview: Drag to resize · double-click to reset
agent_export_pdf: Open in browser to print PDF
api_key_local_only: Keys are stored locally on your device and shown masked in the list.
api_key_base_url_label: Base URL (OpenAI-compatible, required for Agent chat calls)
api_key_fetch_base_url_required: Base URL is required to fetch models
api_key_fetch_key_required: API key is required to fetch models
mcp_dialog_desc: Configure MCP server connection. stdio for local processes, SSE/HTTP for remote services.
mcp_env_placeholder:
One KEY=VALUE per line
e.g. API_TOKEN=xxx
mcp_disabled_hint: When disabled, this server will not be connected.
mcp_page_desc: Manage Model Context Protocol server connections to extend model tool sets.
mcp_builtin_desc: Tools that ship with the app. Install Playwright once to enable it; Web Search and Web Fetch run locally with no install. Enabled tools can be selected per agent.
mcp_empty_hint: Paste an existing MCP JSON config or create one manually. Local stdio servers work best in the desktop app.
mcp_import_desc: Paste an MCP config. Supports the standard "mcpServers" object, the VS Code "servers" shape, or a single server object.
mcp_import_none: No valid servers found in the JSON.
mcp_import_conflict_duplicate: "{{name}}" is duplicated in the JSON, kept the first one.
mcp_inspector_desc: Connect to the server and inspect its tools, resources and prompts.
mcp_inspector_desktop_only: The inspector needs to spawn processes / open connections, so it only works in the desktop app, not the browser dev server.
mcp_inspector_no_tools_desc: This server does not expose any tools.
mcp_inspector_no_resources_desc: This server does not expose any resources.
mcp_inspector_no_prompts_desc: This server does not expose any prompts.
mcp_inspector_read_resource_hint: Select a resource and read it to inspect the returned content.
mcp_empty_desc: Add a stdio / SSE / HTTP server to enable tool call capabilities.
providers_desc: Manage model provider connections: Volcengine, New API, LiteLLM, DMX, and custom API Keys. Select a provider first when adding a new connection.
providers_cors_warning: In browser dev mode, cross-origin requests are blocked. Use the desktop app (pnpm tauri:dev) for fetch features.
provider_desc_volcengine: Fetch enabled models and Ark API Keys via AK/SK
provider_desc_new_api: OpenAI-compatible gateway with Base URL + API Key
provider_desc_litellm: OpenAI-compatible proxy with Base URL + API Key
provider_desc_dmxapi: OpenAI-compatible aggregation platform with Base URL + API Key
provider_desc_manual: Manually enter API Key and available models with OpenAI-compatible calls
gw_dialog_desc: OpenAI-compatible gateway. Enter Base URL and API Key, stored locally only.
gw_empty_desc: Enter Base URL and API Key to start fetching available models.
gw_models_hint: Click "Fetch Models" to get the available model list.
gw_provider_suffix: , fetches models via /v1/models for use in Agent chat.
volc_dialog_desc: AK/SK for fetching enabled models and Ark API Keys, stored locally only.
volc_project_hint: Ark project the keys/endpoints belong to. Required for fetching API Keys. Usually 'default'.
volc_desc: Enter AK/SK to automatically fetch enabled models and Ark API Keys for Agent chat testing.
volc_empty_desc: Add your AccessKey / SecretKey to start fetching enabled models.
volc_models_empty: Click "Fetch Models" to get enabled model list.
volc_keys_desc: Ark API Keys (Bearer) for model inference calls
volc_keys_empty: Click "Fetch API Keys" to save keys for inference.
manual_desc: Manually enter API Key and available models (OpenAI-compatible) for direct Agent chat use.
manual_empty_desc: Add your first provider key and configure available models.
skills_desc: Manage reusable skills and sync to global or project-level Agent directories.
skill_empty_desc: Create your first skill, describe its purpose and add tags for easy retrieval.
skill_project_empty_desc: Configure project-level Skill directories for repositories.
skill_dialog_desc: Define the skill's name, purpose and tags for on-demand model invocation.
skill_disabled_hint: When disabled, the model will not call this skill.
skill_description_placeholder: What this skill does, when to use it...
skill_tags_placeholder: Comma or space separated, e.g. search, web
skill_content_placeholder: Descriptive skills can include detailed instructions; script skills can contain shell scripts run by the Agent sandbox.
skill_project_dialog_desc: Configure project path, Agent and Skill combinations.
skill_market_desc: Browse and install skills from GitHub repositories or the skills.sh marketplace.
skill_market_github_hint: Enter a GitHub repository (owner/repo, optionally owner/repo@ref or a full URL) to list its SKILL.md files.
skill_market_skills_sh_hint: Search the skills.sh marketplace. Content is fetched from the underlying GitHub repository.
skill_market_unsupported_source: This listing points to {{host}}, which skills.sh only indexes for discovery — it exposes no downloadable skill files, so it can't be installed automatically. The same skill is usually available from an installable listing: try searching "lark" instead of "feishu".
skill_market_token_hint: Stored locally. Raises GitHub's rate limit for browsing and installing (60/h when unauthenticated).
skill_requires_not_found: Not found on PATH — install and authenticate it separately
skill_updates_desc: Re-check market skills against their source and update the ones that changed.
tool_escape_input_placeholder: Enter raw text to escape to JSON string literal (newline→\n, quote→\")
tool_json_placeholder: Paste JSON, supports escaped / double-encoded nested fields
tool_encode_placeholder: Enter text to encode, e.g. Hello → \u0048\u0065\u006c\u006c\u006f
tool_url_encode_placeholder: Enter text to encode, e.g. https://a.com/?q=hello world
tool_base64_encode_placeholder: Enter text to encode, e.g. Hello World
tool_markdown_placeholder: Enter Markdown, rendered live on the right
tool_text_editor_search_placeholder: Find text in the current buffer
tool_text_editor_placeholder: Paste text here to keep it as plain text, or open a TXT, log, Markdown, or code text file for quick viewing.
tool_translate_no_model: No available model. Configure a chat model first.
unified_desc: Expose all connected models as a local OpenAI / Anthropic compatible service for Codex, Claude Code and local agents.
unified_no_desktop: Local service requires the desktop (Tauri) environment. In browser dev mode you can preview configuration and docs, but cannot start the HTTP service.
unified_start_msg: Start the service to begin listening for local requests
unified_restart_hint: Changing the port requires stopping and restarting the service
unified_no_models_desc: Connect a provider and fetch models in "Model Providers" first.
unified_search_placeholder: Search model name / real model / connection...
unified_no_match: No models match. Try adjusting filters.
unified_io_import_done: Imported {{added}} connection(s), merged {{merged}} model(s), skipped {{skipped}} duplicate(s)
unified_delete_model_desc: Removes "{{model}}" from its connection so it is no longer exposed. You can restore it by re-fetching models.
agent_no_connections_desc: Add credentials, gateway connections or custom API Keys in "Model Providers" first.
agent_browser_warning: Browser mode has limited model requests, SQLite and sandbox capabilities. Use the desktop app for full functionality.
agent_start_session: Send a message to start a persistent session
agent_input_placeholder: Type a message, Enter to send, Shift+Enter for newline
agent_go_to_providers: Go to Model Providers to refresh models
agent_conn_moved_hint: Connection and model moved to the input bar for easier access.
agent_sandbox_hint: Sandbox mode reserved for local tool execution; macOS will prefer Seatbelt-compatible sandbox.
agent_fetch_model_first: Please fetch and select a model first
agent_message_required: Please enter a message or add an attachment
agent_image_prompt_required: Image generation models require a prompt
agent_video_prompt_required: Video generation models require a prompt
agent_no_ark_key: No Ark API Key available. Please fetch keys in Model Providers first.
agent_no_base_url: This key has no Base URL configured, cannot call in Agent chat
agent_fetch_no_base_url: This key has no Base URL configured, cannot fetch models
agent_no_video_query: {{provider}} does not support video task queries
agent_video_still_running:
Video generation task still running.
Task ID: {{taskId}}
agent_tool_call_result: Model initiated a tool call; recorded in tool history.
agent_tool_calls_summary: Model initiated {{count}} tool calls, written to tool records.
agent_debug_edit_agent_hint: Off by default: only your messages are editable. Turn on to edit agent replies in place for debugging, without re-running the turn.
agent_tool_call_recorded: Model initiated a tool call; recorded in Agent chat.
agent_no_connection_desc: Please add credentials, gateway connections or custom API Keys in "Model Providers" first.
agent_browser_mode_warning: Browser mode has limited model requests, SQLite and sandbox capabilities. Use the desktop app for full functionality.
agent_chat_start_hint: Send a message to start a persistent session
agent_welcome_greeting: What can I help you build?
agent_welcome_pick_model: Pick a model from the bar below to get started
agent_starter_tools_text: List the tools, skills, and MCP servers you can use right now, and what each one is good for.
agent_starter_code_text: Write a TypeScript function that debounces an async function, with full types and a usage example.
agent_starter_explain_text: Explain how the Model Context Protocol (MCP) works in simple terms, with a concrete example.
agent_starter_plan_text: Break down building a small command-line todo app into clear, ordered steps.
agent_textarea_placeholder: Type a message, Enter to send, Shift+Enter for newline
agent_locked_hint: Agent is locked once the conversation has started
agent_research_chat_model_required: ResearchAgent needs a chat-capable model, not an image or video generation model.
agent_research_selector_desc: Auto-switches to Execution write so it can save research files to the workspace.
agent_mcp_errors: Some MCP servers failed to load: {{servers}}
agent_mcp_pending: Still connecting to MCP servers (skipped this turn, retry shortly): {{servers}}
agent_tools_need_gateway: Tools (skills / MCP) are enabled, but the current model isn't exposed through the local Unified gateway. Start the gateway and enable this model on the Unified page, then try again.
agent_gateway_unavailable: The local Unified gateway isn't running, so tool calls are unavailable. Start it on the Unified page first.
agent_checkpoint_approval_required: The agent is paused until you approve or reject this step.
agent_checkpoint_auto_approve_hint: When enabled, ResearchAgent checkpoints are approved automatically for this session. Protected tools still run under the current sandbox mode.
agent_checkpoint_auto_approve_card_hint: Turn on to approve this and future checkpoints in this session.
agent_checkpoint_auto_approved_note: Auto-approved by the session auto-approval setting.
agent_checkpoint_already_pending: Another checkpoint is already waiting for approval
agent_ask_input_required: The agent is paused until you respond.
agent_ask_already_pending: Another question is already waiting for an answer
agent_leaked_checkpoint_tool_call: The model wrote the Checkpoint tool call as plain text, so no real approval card was opened. Retry this turn so ResearchAgent can request approval through the real checkpoint tool.
agents_manage_desc: Create reusable agents with custom system prompts, models, internal tools, skills and MCP.
agents_install_desc: Install a framework-based external agent (Python / Node) from a local directory, build its isolated environment, and add it to the agent list. The directory must contain agent.json.
agents_install_folder_hint: Pick an agent package directory that contains agent.json.
agents_install_github_hint: Enter owner/repo, owner/repo/sub/dir, or a GitHub URL. The folder must contain agent.json.
agents_update_build_failed: Failed to rebuild the environment during update.
agents_install_success: Installed and added to the agent list.
agents_empty: No custom agents yet. Click above to create one.
agents_dialog_desc: Configure this agent's system prompt, model and available tools.
agents_system_prompt_placeholder: You are a helpful coding assistant…
agents_no_models: No gateway models available. Start the gateway and enable models on the Unified page first.
agents_model_hint: Models are routed through the local Unified gateway.
agents_internal_tools_hint: bash and file tools (subject to the sandbox mode).
agents_workspace_hint: Execution root for internal tools. When unset, defaults to ~/.llmtoolforge/sessions/<sessionId> as this session's workspace.
agent_conn_model_hint: Connection and model controls have moved to the input bar.
agent_refresh_models_hint: Go to Model Providers to refresh models
